subtasks: Generate minimal unit tests for critical behaviors
tests:
Certainly! Here are three high-risk failure modes for the task **"Return the sum of the main diagonal"** of a matrix, along with short rationales:

1. **Incorrect indexing of diagonal elements**  
   *Rationale:* The main diagonal elements are those where the row and column indices are equal (i.e., matrix[i][i]). Using incorrect indices (e.g., matrix[i][j] with i ≠ j) will lead to summing the wrong

diagnostics.failure_modes:
Certainly! Here are three high-risk failure modes for the task **"Return the sum of the main diagonal"** of a matrix, along with short rationales:

1. **Incorrect indexing of diagonal elements**  
   *Rationale:* The main diagonal elements are those where the row and column indices are equal (i.e., matrix[i][i]). Using incorrect indices (e.g., matrix[i][j] with i ≠ j) will lead to summing the wrong;max constraints: extremely large inputs;degenerate: empty or single-element
